recent_developments.title: Law on Personal Data Protection takes effect
recent_developments.summary: Vietnam's PDP Law came into force, elevating Decree 13's framework to statute with revenue-based fines and an explicit ban on personal data trading.

recent_developments.title: Commission proposes Digital Omnibus including GDPR amendments
recent_developments.summary: The European Commission's Digital Omnibus package proposed targeted GDPR simplifications (records exemptions, breach-reporting streamlining, clarified AI/legitimate-interest provisions), drawing significant scrutiny from privacy advocates and the EDPB.

recent_developments.title: DPDP Rules, 2025 notified
recent_developments.summary: MeitY notified the final Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025, setting phased compliance timelines (most substantive obligations apply 12-18 months from notification) and operational detail on consent managers, breach reporting and children's data.

recent_developments.title: GAID 2025 takes effect
recent_developments.summary: The NDPC's General Application and Implementation Directive became operative, formalising registration tiers, audit-filing deadlines and DPO credentialing under the NDPA.

recent_developments.title: CJEU ruling refines pseudonymisation and personal-data scope
recent_developments.summary: The Court of Justice (EDPS v SRB) clarified when pseudonymised data ceases to be personal data in the hands of a recipient without means of re-identification.

recent_developments.title: Amendment No. 13 takes effect
recent_developments.summary: The most significant reform since 1996 became operative: GDPR-style definitions, PPA administrative fines (potentially reaching millions of shekels for large databases), expanded investigative powers and DPO mandates.

recent_developments.title: Personal Data Protection Commission established
recent_developments.summary: Taiwan's independent PDPC formally commenced operations as the dedicated data protection authority, consolidating enforcement previously spread across sector ministries.

recent_developments.title: European Commission renews UK adequacy decisions
recent_developments.summary: The Commission adopted renewed UK adequacy decisions (to December 2031) after assessing the Data (Use and Access) Act, preserving free EU-UK data flows.

recent_developments.title: National Assembly passes the PDP Law
recent_developments.summary: The National Assembly approved the Law on Personal Data Protection with near-unanimous support, setting a 1 January 2026 effective date.

recent_developments.title: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 receives Royal Assent
recent_developments.summary: DUAA became law after extended parliamentary ping-pong over AI/copyright amendments, with staged commencement through 2025-26.

recent_developments.title: Statutory tort for serious invasions of privacy commences
recent_developments.summary: The new cause of action allowing individuals to sue for serious invasions of privacy (intrusion or misuse of information) took effect under the 2024 amendment.

recent_developments.title: Breach notification and DPO obligations commence
recent_developments.summary: The final phase of the 2024 Amendment Act took effect, requiring notification of significant breaches to the Commissioner (within 72 hours) and affected individuals, and DPO appointment above processing thresholds.

recent_developments.title: SDAIA expands PDPL guidance suite
recent_developments.summary: SDAIA continued releasing guidance (DPO, DPIA, consent, disclosure cases) and signalled the first adequacy/whitelist assessments for cross-border transfers.

recent_developments.title: Turnover-based leak fines take effect
recent_developments.summary: The new administrative penalty scale for personal data breaches became effective, dramatically raising financial exposure for operators suffering repeat leaks.

recent_developments.title: DPA amendment bills advance in Congress
recent_developments.summary: Proposals to raise penalties, strengthen NPC enforcement powers and align the DPA with newer international norms continued through committee in the 19th/20th Congress.
